title: Stop the consistency check from false-failing mid-delivery
summary: The whole-binder consistency check decides an item is done by looking for its touched files on disk, so an item that only edits an existing file looks finished before it is built — and a check run mid-delivery can halt a wave over wording that has not been written yet. This makes the check treat an item as landed only once its git done ref exists, and moves the single-item build's run of the check to after the merge, so it only ever judges items that have truly landed.
motivation: The check must implement the landed-item contract the docs already state, so a mid-delivery run never false-halts a wave.

included: check_shared_terms.py — replace the disk-existence proxy for 'item delivered' with a git done-ref probe, keep byte-identity enforcement for landed items, update the module docstring, and extend the embedded --self-test with git-backed fixtures
The two generated mirrors of check_shared_terms.py (.agents/... and plugins/karta/...), regenerated byte-identical by sync_codex_skills.py in the same change
karta-build's 9c-single sequence in SKILL.md — move the shared-terms check to after the merge completes and after the item's done ref is written, and update its halt-path and pending wording to match
The two generated mirrors of karta-build's SKILL.md, regenerated byte-identical by sync_codex_skills.py in the same change
excluded: No new CLI flag or option on the check script — a caller-passed list of landed items was considered and rejected; the script derives landed state itself from the git done refs so no upstream has to feed it
No change to karta-deliver's SKILL — its post-wave shared-terms check already runs after the per-item done refs are written, so it is correct as-is under the new semantics
No README change — the README prose already states the fixed contract ('an item not yet built is skipped until it lands'); this only makes the code match it
No change to validate_binder.py or the binder JSON schema — the plan-time shape check for shared_terms is unaffected; only the deliver-time byte-identity check changes

design_facts.stack: karta plugin repo — Python 3.11+ stdlib scripts run via `uv run --script`, each with an embedded `--self-test` ([PASS]/[FAIL] + `N/N checks passed`); Markdown SKILL.md skills with 3-way byte-identical mirrors (skills/ -> .agents/skills/ -> plugins/karta/skills/) regenerated by sync_codex_skills.py; JSON binder files. Ships as a Claude Code plugin and a Codex CLI plugin.

work_items.title: Judge an item landed by its git done ref, not a file on disk
work_items.summary: The check no longer false-fails on an item that merely edits an existing file — it holds off on that item's wording until git shows the item has actually landed.
work_items.contract: Replace the 'item delivered' test in skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py. Today an item counts as delivered when at least one of its touched files exists under the scan root — an existence proxy that is the bug: an item that edits an existing file always looks delivered, so mid-delivery, before that item lands, the check finds the canonical term absent from the still-old file and reports a false FAIL (SHARED TERMS: DRIFT). Change the rule so an item counts as LANDED only when the git ref refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done resolves. The <slug> comes from the binder's own top-level `slug`; resolve the ref with a subprocess git call (e.g. `git show-ref --verify --quiet refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done`) run with cwd set to the check's scan root. This stays pure stdlib — subprocess is stdlib and git is already a hard karta requirement — so every invocation form keeps behaving identically with nothing to provision. An item without that done ref is PENDING: every shared_terms entry listing it reports [PENDING] with a message naming the new semantics (e.g. 'not yet landed — no done ref'), and is skipped, never a FAIL. A scan root that is not a git repository, or git being unavailable, makes every item PENDING — visible skip lines, never a crash and never a silent pass, and the summary line still prints. For a LANDED item the existing behavior stands: the canonical must appear byte-identical in at least one of the item's existing touched files, and a landed item none of whose touched files exist on the tree cannot render the term and is a FAIL naming that item. Update the module docstring — the current 'PENDING — at least one listed item has no existing touched file' line — to the done-ref semantics. Extend --self-test with git-backed fixtures (a tempdir `git init` plus `git update-ref` to write done refs): the regression case (a touched file exists with the term absent but the item has NO done ref -> PENDING, not FAIL); a landed drift -> FAIL; every listed item landed and byte-identical -> OK; a non-git root -> PENDING, not a crash; a landed item whose touched files are all absent -> FAIL; and every existing case still passing. This is a canonical-plus-mirrors change: edit skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py, then run `uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py` so the two generated copies (.agents/skills/... and plugins/karta/skills/...) regenerate byte-identical in the same change (house.1); all three copies are listed in touches.

oracle.command: uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: check_shared_terms.py --self-test exits 0 and prints an `N/N checks passed` summary with every fixture [PASS] and no [FAIL]
an item whose touched file exists with the canonical absent but with no refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done ref reports [PENDING], not [FAIL] — the exact regression the fix closes
a landed item (done ref present) whose touched files lack the canonical is a [FAIL] naming that item, and every listed item landed and byte-identical is OK
a scan root that is not a git repository, or git being unavailable, makes every listed item [PENDING] without crashing and still prints the summary line
a landed item whose touched files are all absent on the tree is a [FAIL] naming that item
the module docstring describes the done-ref landed semantics and no longer states the old 'no existing touched file' proxy
sync_codex_skills.py --check reports no drift across the two regenerated mirror copies and validate_plugin --self-test passes with all [PASS]

work_items.title: Run the single-item check after the merge lands, not before
work_items.summary: Under the new landed rule a pre-merge check would skip the very item being merged and pass vacuously; running it after the merge and its done ref restores a real check for the direct single-item build.
work_items.contract: Reorder the 9c-single sequence in skills/karta-build/SKILL.md so the shared-terms check runs after the merge completes and after refs/karta/<slug>/item-<item-id>/done is written. Today that check is the step before the merge ('On the re-validated merged result, before the merge completes, run ...'). Under done-ref semantics a pre-merge check would always skip every shared_terms entry that lists the item being merged — its own done ref cannot exist yet — so the check goes vacuous at the exact moment the single-item hatch relies on it; moving it after the done-ref write is what makes it able to see the item it just landed. Keep the check invocation string byte-identical to karta-deliver's post-wave step: `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --binder <binder path>`. Keep the halt-on-FAIL footing — a non-zero exit ([FAIL], a declared shared_terms string drifted between items that both landed) halts on the same footing as a failed post-wave build; the human fixes the wording or reverts the merge — but adjust the halt-path prose to the post-merge reality: the merge and the done ref have already landed, so the halt reverts them rather than writing a failed ref on an un-merged item branch. Update the step's [PENDING] description to the done-ref wording (an item with no done ref yet is skipped), replacing the old 'items not yet delivered' phrasing. Keep every cross-reference to another step by name, never by ordinal number (house.3). Canonical-plus-mirrors change: edit skills/karta-build/SKILL.md, then run `uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py` so the two generated copies regenerate byte-identical in the same change (house.1); all three copies are in touches.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: in the 9c-single sequence, the shared-terms check step appears after the merge step and after the refs/karta/<slug>/item-<item-id>/done write, and no longer before the merge
the check invocation string is byte-identical to karta-deliver's post-wave step: `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --binder <binder path>`
the step's [PENDING] description uses the done-ref wording (an item with no done ref yet is skipped), not the old 'items not yet delivered' phrasing
the halt-path prose reflects the post-merge reality (reverting the landed merge and done ref) and no cross-reference to another step is by ordinal number
sync_codex_skills.py --check reports no drift across the two regenerated mirror copies and validate_plugin --self-test passes with all [PASS]
